metadata.greeting: This weekend looks perfect for getting out and about! I've found a mix of free museum activities and exciting outdoor adventures in East London. Get ready for some hands-on fun and creative play!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Road, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
activities.description: Explore interactive spaces including sensory playscapes, a finger skateboard park, an 'Imagination Playground' construction zone, and a performance and story-telling stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind, offering hands-on activities and creative exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground from Hackney Downs to Cambridge Heath.
transport.tip: Check out the family-friendly cafe with bottle warming facilities.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the vast outdoor space, including playgrounds and walking trails. Plenty of room to run around and explore.
activities.whyItWorks: Massive outdoor space perfect for toddlers to burn energy. Close to Westfield Stratford for easy access to amenities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy in the park.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: Enjoy a large park with playgrounds, a lake, and open spaces for running and playing.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of open space and structured play areas, suitable for a range of ages and energy levels.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus towards Mile End or Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for special Easter activities.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore historical homes through the ages, with hands-on activities and a beautiful enclosed garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor exploration and outdoor play, with interactive elements to engage young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus towards Old Street or Liverpool Street.
transport.tip: Pick up an activity sheet or explorer backpack from the front desk.

activities.title: Spitalfields Market
activities.price: Free to enter, cost for activities
activities.description: Explore the market with family-friendly highlights, including potential Easter activities.
activities.whyItWorks: The vibrant atmosphere and open space is great for a stroll, with potential for engaging activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus towards Liverpool Street or Shoreditch High Street.
transport.tip: Check for specific Easter events or activities closer to the date.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals and gardens. A fun and educational experience for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see animals up close and learn about farming in an urban setting.

transport.fromHackney: Local - check bus routes
transport.tip: A great way to spend a morning or afternoon.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for toddlers, offering a safe and engaging play environment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: A great option for younger children.

activities.title: 'Driving' the DLR
activities.description: Ride the driverless DLR and sit at the front to pretend you're driving.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun and unique way to see parts of London, with the novelty of sitting where the driver would be.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to a DLR interchange station like Stratford.
transport.tip: Avoid peak hours for a more comfortable ride.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur skeletons, fascinating exhibits, and interactive displays at this iconic museum.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a captivating and educational experience with plenty to see and do for all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Discover interactive exhibits and iconic objects, including space exploration artifacts.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging displays that make learning about science fun for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check out the Wonderlab for hands-on experiments (ticketed).

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.description: Explore art, design, and culture from around the world, with family activities available.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a range of activities for families, encouraging play and learning through the museum's collection.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for family trails and backpacks.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Visit a small zoo with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a free and accessible way to see animals in a park setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the Overground to Golders Green. Then a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.ageRange: Children only (adults must be accompanied by a child)
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and engaging space for children to play and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus to Russell Square or Holborn, then walk.
transport.tip: Perfect for a summer day.

activities.title: Hampstead Heath
activities.description: Explore the large, hilly parkland with playgrounds and swimming ponds.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a great outdoor space for running around and exploring nature.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath or Gospel Oak.
transport.tip: Bring a picnic and enjoy the views of London.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A hub of arts, fun and food, offering concerts, festivals, creative workshops, and hands-on installations.
activities.whyItWorks: Welcoming and accessible, packed with energy, and offers free activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Walk across the bridge.
transport.tip: Check the website for free events and family activities.

activities.title: British Library
activities.description: Explore the world of fairy tales or participate in the Story Explorers program.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers an immersive journey through the world of fairy tales with exciting works from its collection.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or Overground to Highbury and Islington, then the Victoria line to Euston
transport.tip: Check the website for family-friendly events and workshops

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Discover illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a familiar story with outdoor exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Walthamstow
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance!

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Explore the gardens and historic surrounds while hunting for Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues.
activities.whyItWorks: A perfect day out for the family with a fun hunt through the gardens.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Waterloo, then train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to avoid disappointment.

activities.title: London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids will love spotting animals and searching for eggs.

transport.fromHackney: Bus and tube to Regent's Park
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of adventure play, animal encounters, and an Easter-themed trail.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow, then bus.
transport.tip: Allow a full day to explore everything.

activities.title: Easter at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Enjoy Easter-themed egg hunts and seasonal workshops amidst beautiful blooms.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with fun family activities.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Battersea Park Children's Zoo
activities.description: Visit the zoo for Easter activities and animal encounters.
activities.whyItWorks: A smaller zoo that's perfect for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Train and bus to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Check for special Easter events.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Easter Egg trail around the historic ship.
activities.whyItWorks: Mix of history and chocolate.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Greenwich
transport.tip: Explore Greenwich Park after

activities.title: Luminarium: Myriad
activities.description: A glowing, inflatable maze of colour and light that feels part art installation, part sensory playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive, sensory experience that kids will love.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Woolwich
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance
